info.name: Verbex
info.description: Postman collection for the Verbex REST API Server - a comprehensive inverted index implementation with REST endpoints for index management, document operations, and search functionality.

request.description: Basic health check at root endpoint. Returns server health status, version, and timestamp.
Versioned health check endpoint. Returns server health status, version, and timestamp.

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Authentication endpoints for login and token validation

request.description: Authenticate with username and password. TenantId is optional - set to a tenant identifier for tenant-scoped authentication, or null/omit for global admin authentication. Default credentials: admin/password
Validate the current bearer token. Returns whether the token is valid.

item.name: Indices
item.description: Index management endpoints for creating, listing, and deleting indices

request.description: Get a list of all available indices with their configurations.

body.raw:
{
    "Id": "test-index",
    "Name": "Test Index",
    "Description": "A test index for demonstration",
    "InMemory": true,
    "StorageMode": "MemoryOnly",
    "EnableLemmatizer": false,
    "EnableStopWordRemover": false,
    "MinTokenLength": 0,
    "MaxTokenLength": 0,
    "Labels": ["demo", "test"],
    "Tags": {
        "environment": "development",
        "owner": "api-team"
    }
}

request.description:
Create a new index with the specified configuration.

Required fields:
- Id: Unique identifier for the index
- Name: Display name for the index

Optional fields:
- Description: Description of the index
- RepositoryFilename: Filename for persistence (default: {Id}.db)
- InMemory: Whether to keep index in memory (default: false)
- StorageMode: Storage mode - MemoryOnly, DiskOnly, or Hybrid (default: MemoryOnly)
- EnableLemmatizer: Enable lemmatization (default: false)
- EnableStopWordRemover: Enable stop word removal (default: false)
- MinTokenLength: Minimum token length (default: 0)
- MaxTokenLength: Maximum token length (default: 0)
- Labels: Array of string labels for categorization (optional)
- Tags: Key-value pairs for custom metadata (optional)

body.raw:
{
    "Id": "advanced-index",
    "Name": "Advanced Index",
    "Description": "An index with advanced text processing options",
    "RepositoryFilename": "advanced-index.db",
    "InMemory": false,
    "StorageMode": "Hybrid",
    "EnableLemmatizer": true,
    "EnableStopWordRemover": true,
    "MinTokenLength": 2,
    "MaxTokenLength": 50,
    "Labels": ["production", "searchable", "nlp-enabled"],
    "Tags": {
        "owner": "search-team",
        "version": "2.0",
        "tier": "premium"
    }
}

request.description: Create a new index with advanced text processing options including lemmatization, stop word removal, token length constraints, labels, and tags.
Get detailed statistics and information about a specific index.
Check if an index exists. Returns 200 if the index exists, 404 if not found. No response body is returned.
Delete an existing index and all its documents.

Rebuild the term ID cache for an index. Use this if the database has been modified externally or to force a cache reload.

Response includes:
- Success: Whether the rebuild was successful
- TermsLoaded: Number of terms loaded into the cache
- IndexId: The index identifier

item.name: Documents
item.description: Document management endpoints for adding, retrieving, and deleting documents within indices

request.description: Get a list of all documents in the specified index.

query.description: Comma-separated list of document IDs to retrieve

request.description:
Retrieve multiple documents by ID in a single request. This is more efficient than making multiple individual document requests.

Query parameters:
- ids: Comma-separated list of document IDs to retrieve

Response includes:
- Documents: Array of found documents with full metadata
- NotFound: Array of document IDs that were not found
- Count: Number of documents returned
- RequestedCount: Total number of document IDs that were requested

Add a new document to the specified index. The document ID will be auto-generated if not provided.

Required fields:
- Content: The document content to be indexed

Optional fields:
- Id: Custom document ID (GUID format)
- Labels: Array of string labels for categorization
- Tags: Key-value pairs for custom metadata
Add a new document with a custom document ID (GUID format), labels, and tags.
Retrieve a specific document by its ID from the specified index.
Check if a document exists in the specified index. Returns 200 if the document exists, 404 if not found. No response body is returned.
Delete a specific document from the specified index.

query.description: Comma-separated list of document IDs to delete

request.description:
Delete multiple documents from an index by their IDs. This is more efficient than making multiple individual delete requests.

Query parameters:
- ids: Comma-separated list of document IDs to delete

Response includes:
- Deleted: Array of document IDs that were deleted
- NotFound: Array of document IDs that were not found
- DeletedCount: Number of documents deleted
- NotFoundCount: Number of documents not found
- RequestedCount: Total number of IDs requested

Add multiple documents to an index in a single request. This is more efficient than making multiple individual add requests.

Request body:
- Documents: Array of document objects
  - Id (optional): Custom document ID (auto-generated if not provided)
  - Name: Document name/path (required)
  - Content: Document content to index (required)
  - Labels (optional): Array of string labels
  - Tags (optional): Key-value pairs
  - CustomMetadata (optional): Any JSON value

Response includes:
- Added: Array of successfully added documents
- Failed: Array of documents that failed to be added
- AddedCount: Number of documents added
- FailedCount: Number of documents that failed
- RequestedCount: Total number of documents in request

Check if multiple documents exist in an index. This is useful for validating document IDs before performing operations.

Request body:
- Ids: Array of document IDs to check

Response includes:
- Exists: Array of document IDs that exist
- NotFound: Array of document IDs that do not exist
- ExistsCount: Number of documents that exist
- NotFoundCount: Number of documents that do not exist
- RequestedCount: Total number of IDs in request

item.name: Search
item.description: Search endpoints for querying indexed documents

request.description:
Search for documents in the specified index using the provided query.

Required fields:
- Query: The search query string

Optional fields:
- MaxResults: Maximum number of results to return (default: 100)
Search for documents with a limited number of results returned.
Search for documents filtered by labels. Documents must have ALL specified labels (AND logic). Label matching is case-insensitive.
Search for documents filtered by tags (key-value pairs). Documents must have ALL specified tags with matching values (AND logic). Tag matching is exact (case-sensitive).
Search for documents filtered by both labels and tags. Documents must match the search query AND have all specified labels AND have all specified tags.

item.name: Backup & Restore
item.description: Backup and restore endpoints for creating and restoring index backups

request.description:
Create a backup of an index. Returns a ZIP archive containing the index database and metadata.

The backup archive (.vbx) contains:
- manifest.json: Backup version, timestamp, checksums, and statistics
- metadata.json: Index configuration and settings
- index.db: SQLite database file

Note: Only on-disk indices can be backed up. In-memory indices will return an error.

formdata.description: Optional: Name for the restored index (defaults to original name)

request.description:
Restore a backup as a new index.

Form parameters:
- file (required): The backup file (.vbx)
- name (optional): Name for the restored index (defaults to original name)
- indexId (optional): Custom ID for the restored index

If an index with the same name already exists, a unique name will be generated (e.g., 'myindex_restored_20240115103000').

Restored indices are always on-disk (not in-memory).

Restore a backup by replacing an existing index.

Form parameters:
- file (required): The backup file (.vbx)

This operation:
1. Validates the backup file
2. Flushes and closes the existing index
3. Creates a backup of the current database
4. Replaces the database with the backup
5. Verifies the checksum
6. Reopens the index

If verification fails, the original database is restored automatically.

Note: Only on-disk indices can be replaced. In-memory indices will return an error.
